notes: CardScanR tracked history starts from the first tracked build for each card.
Images are referenced by URL and are not mirrored into this cache yet.
This describes the CardScanR internal data API, not a public developer platform.
Images are referenced by URL only and are not mirrored into this cache yet.
Consumer-facing summary of the internal app data layer constraints.
Static remote app settings used by CardScanR.
Catalogue card records store image URLs only.
Use imageSmall, imageLarge, imageSource, and imageCached: false.
Japanese catalogue card records may include null values for metadata that is not present in set-level TCGdex responses.
English Pokemon catalogue sets are built from official PokemonTCG API endpoints.
Japanese Pokemon catalogue sets are built from official TCGdex REST endpoints using the ja upstream language path.
App-facing metadata for a card image reference.
Images are cached by exact card identity, not by card name alone.
Provider image IDs are references and should resolve to a CardScanR card identity.
Public timestamps are UTC; Flutter converts times locally.
Binary image files are not included in this cache.
Future CardScanR CDN image URLs can be added later beside provider references.
Language-specific app-facing UTC price freshness metadata.
Includes expected update cadence and batch metadata for EN local-first rotation.
Current price cache entry.
Per-set current price files are latest-known snapshots and may be overwritten each build.
At least one of marketPrice, lowPrice, or highPrice should be numeric when present.
Currency is stored per price record.
Use fetchedAtUtc and staleness fields for app card-detail freshness messaging.
Japanese price files are only emitted when official/free source pricing is actually available.
Pokewallet JP CardMarket prices are EUR and TCGPlayer prices are USD.
Do not combine or convert EUR and USD into a single price.
Pokewallet prices are overseas market guide prices, not Australian sold prices.
Apps should display source and currency for Pokewallet prices.
Currency conversion is an app-layer feature for later and is not performed in the cache.
English Pokemon current prices by set are built from PokemonTCG API card pricing fields.
Japanese Pokemon current prices by set are optional and only appear when official/free source pricing is available.
These files are latest-known current snapshots, not lifetime/all-time price history.
App card detail freshness should combine set-level and record-level freshness timestamps.
Manual refresh should check cache first and never overwrite valid values with no-result/error data.
Tracked historical movement remains limited to CardScanR-tracked cards.
JP Pokewallet set files may use top-level currency mixed when record currencies include both EUR and USD.
Record-level currency is authoritative for JP Pokewallet records.
JP nextExpectedPriceUpdateAtUtc may be null until a regular JP refresh cadence exists.
Daily snapshots only for tracked cards from data/cards_to_track.json for now.
This is append-by-day history, not a full all-card historical archive.
Build telemetry for current cache and CardScanR tracked history generation.
Backwards compatible fields id/url/sha256 remain required.
Richer metadata may include game, language, schemaVersion, and recommendedCacheTtlSeconds.
Repository-level image cache policy for app and future CDN use.
The cache key rule is {game}|{language}|{setId}|{collectorNumber}|{normalizedName}|{variant}.
binaryImagesStored must remain false while this repository stores metadata and references only.
Controlled Pokewallet JP price build diagnostics without secrets or raw payload dumps.
Includes set-map probing metrics, set-detail metrics, fallback search metrics, and confidence-gated write outcomes.
Diagnostics for the Pokewallet provider catalogue foundation builder.
The builder stores safe metadata only and must not store full provider responses.
Image checks record endpoint availability metadata only.
Full catalogue mode is resumable and writes provider-catalog per-set files with image references only.
Resumable provider metadata export for one Pokewallet set.
Files live at /provider-catalog/pokewallet/cards/{language}/{setId}.json.
Records store provider IDs, set data, card numbers, selected names, variant labels, image endpoint references, and field-key lists only.
Image files are not stored.
Provider metadata is not official CardScanR canonical catalogue data yet.
CardScanR may use it for scan and match assistance after review.
Pricing integration is separate.
Research-stage provider catalogue card sample for future CardScanR matching.
Card records store provider IDs, set IDs, language, card number, selected names, variant labels, image endpoint references, and field-key lists only.
No image binaries or full provider responses are stored.
Research-stage Pokewallet provider catalogue summaries.
Image files are not stored in the repository; image availability samples store response metadata only.
Full catalogue summary fields report per-set file counts, cards written by language, and state path.
Production catalogue integration comes later after provider metadata coverage is reviewed.
Diagnostics-only probe for the Pokewallet Pro /prices/:setCode endpoint.
The probe must not write current price cache files.
Pokewallet prices are overseas market guide prices.
TCGPlayer usually represents US market data and is commonly USD.
CardMarket usually represents European market data and is commonly EUR.
These records are not Australian sold prices.
Apps should display source and currency; currency conversion belongs in the app layer later.
True Australian market pricing should come from eBay AU sold listings or local sales data later.
Diagnostics-only Pokewallet Pro trial discovery across set details, prices, statistics, completion values, trending sets, top cards, price history samples, and image metadata samples.
The probe must not write current price cache files or store image binaries.
Pokewallet prices are overseas market guide prices.
TCGPlayer usually represents US market data and is commonly USD.
CardMarket usually represents European market data and is commonly EUR.
These records are not Australian sold prices.
Apps should display source and currency; currency conversion belongs in the app layer later.
Pokewallet image endpoints require an API key; discovery records only status, content type, and content length.
Resumable local state for diagnostics-only Pokewallet Pro trial discovery.
Contains counters and completed endpoint keys only; no provider secrets or full provider payloads.
Top-level app-facing UTC status summary for current price freshness.
Contains language-level status, staleness, and expected update metadata.
CardScanR tracked history means history since CardScanR started tracking the card.
This is not lifetime/all-time market history.
